task_completion.description: Launch the Weasis DICOM Viewer application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis DICOM Viewer application window is open and visible on the screen.
A file explorer window or the Weasis import/open dialog is visible, clearly showing the current path as '/home/ga/DICOM/samples' or '~/DICOM/samples'.

task_completion.description: Select a DICOM file from the samples directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file within the 'samples' directory is highlighted, selected, or being dragged into the Weasis application.

task_completion.description: Initiate the loading of the selected DICOM file into Weasis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The selected DICOM file appears in the Weasis DICOM explorer/study list panel, or an import dialog/progress bar is visible indicating the file is being loaded.

task_completion.description: Successfully display the DICOM image in the Weasis viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main viewing area of Weasis displays the loaded DICOM image, which should appear as a synthetic circle pattern. Metadata on the screen may also indicate a 'CT' modality.

integrity.description: Verify that the image is opened in the Weasis DICOM Viewer and not a different application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Weasis-specific UI elements, branding, menus, and layout. Ensure it is not a generic image viewer or web browser.

integrity.description: Verify that the opened file is the correct synthetic DICOM file provided in the environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The displayed image must show the synthetic circle pattern. It should not be a real medical scan or a random image downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the GUI to accomplish the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the progression of GUI interactions (opening menus, clicking dialogs, or drag-and-drop) rather than just a terminal window executing a script to bypass the UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target DICOM files are located exactly at '/home/ga/DICOM/samples'. These files are synthetically generated and contain a distinct circle pattern as their pixel data. The DICOM Modality attribute for these files is hardcoded to 'CT'.
